one_line_takeaway: SafeRedir shows that robust concept unlearning for diffusion models can be done as an external, 50MB inference-time wrapper that redirects only the unsafe tokens in embedding space — matching or beating weight-editing baselines on forgetting and beating them on benign preservation and prompt-manipulation robustness, while transferring zero-shot across SD variants.
key_finding: SafeRedir achieves the best or near-best forgetting while best preserving benign content and being the most robust to prompt-based reactivation, at negligible cost. It reaches FSR 99.84% (NSFW), 97.00% (Van Gogh, second to AdvUnlearn's 97.10%), and 96.80% (Church, best). It simultaneously attains the lowest CSDR on NSFW (6.68%) and Van Gogh (5.72%) and the highest person-retention PDR (95.60%, nearly matching the 96.56% original model). For robustness it gives the lowest common-attack ASR on I2P (0.70%) and near-lowest on MMA (1.73% vs AdvUnlearn 1.03%), and under UnlearnDiffAtk the lowest ASR on Van Gogh (50.16%) and Church (3.12%); even the adaptive detector-aware attack stays at 1.56-7.81% ASR. It is only 50MB and adds <1.5% inference latency for unsafe prompts, and a module trained on SD v1.4 transfers zero-shot to six SD variants at >94% FSR.
core_contribution: SafeRedir is a plug-and-play, inference-time framework for robust concept unlearning in text-to-image diffusion models that operates entirely in the prompt-embedding space and never modifies the U-Net, text encoder, or VAE weights. Its distinct contribution (Sec. 4, not to be confused with the ESD/UCE/MACE/AdvUnlearn/SafeGen baselines surveyed in Sec. 2.2) is a two-stage 'identify-then-redirect' design: (1) a multi-modal safety detector that fuses the prompt embedding, the step-t image latent z_t, and the diffusion timestep t via cross-attention to decide, at each denoising step, whether the current generative state is unsafe; and (2) a token-level redirector that, upon detection, shifts only the unsafe tokens across a learned 'safe boundary' using three predicted per-token factors — a normalized direction vector (Delta-tilde), a soft mask m localizing which tokens to edit, and an adaptive per-token scaling alpha in [0,1] — combined as p_hat = p_emb + alpha * (Delta_filtered/||Delta_filtered||) * ||p_emb||. The framework is trained end-to-end with paired safe/unsafe prompts that share benign context, using a combined classification + MSE + cosine + mask-BCE + embedding-alignment loss. The key insight is that unsafe semantics are distributed across the embedding space rather than isolated in keywords, so an embedding-space intervention generalizes to paraphrased and adversarial prompts better than keyword- or weight-level erasure.
threat_model: Inference-time, prompt-based reactivation of erased concepts (Sec. 2.3). The adversary has black-box query access to a deployed diffusion model and crafts arbitrary prompts — including paraphrases, synonym substitutions, implicit descriptions, and optimization-based adversarial/jailbreak prompts (UnlearnDiffAtk, MMA-Diffusion) — to force generation of forbidden content (nudity, copyrighted artist style, target objects). The defender ('model governor') has full model access and deploys SafeRedir as an external wrapper to (i) prevent recovery of erased concepts and (ii) preserve quality on benign prompts. The paper also evaluates a defense-aware ADAPTIVE attacker that adds a detector-evasion term to the attack loss (Eq. 15).
limitations: Scope is restricted to Stable-Diffusion-compatible backbones that share prompt-embedding, timestep, and cross-attention conditioning interfaces; the authors explicitly leave extension to architecturally different conditioning mechanisms as future work. Robustness is uneven across concept types: under the UnlearnDiffAtk adversarial setting, SafeRedir's own NSFW ASR is 9.38% (worse than AdvUnlearn's 4.69%) and its Van Gogh ASR is 50.16% — 'best' among baselines but still meaning roughly half of adversarial style prompts succeed, reflecting weak disentanglement of abstract/global stylistic concepts (also flagged as future work). The method depends on the quality of auto-constructed paired safe/unsafe training data and on the detector firing correctly; false-positive redirection that suppresses benign requests is acknowledged as a risk in the ethics section. Robustness numbers are reported on the authors' chosen attack suite, and the detector adds a small (though bounded) latency and 50MB footprint per deployment.

datasets_used: Auto-constructed prompt-image pairs (paired safe/unsafe prompts) for training
IGMU (matched unsafe/benign prompts) for standard evaluation
MMA / MMA-Diffusion (adversarial NSFW prompts) for robustness evaluation
Six SD-family variants for transfer: SD v1.5, Anything-v3.0, Dreamlike v1.0, OpenJourney v1, Realistic Vision v1.4, Waifu Diffusion v1.3
Tasks/concepts: NSFW nudity, Van Gogh style, Church object
quantitative_results: Forgetting FSR (Table 5): SafeRedir NSFW 99.84% (best), Van Gogh 97.00% (2nd to AdvUnlearn 97.10%), Church 96.80% (best)
Preservation CSDR (Table 6): SafeRedir NSFW 6.68% (best), Van Gogh 5.72% (best), Church 6.83% (2nd to UCE 5.83%)
Preservation LPIPS (Table 6): SafeRedir Van Gogh 0.20 (best), Church 0.11 (best), NSFW 0.23
Person Detect Rate (Fig. 10): SafeRedir 95.60% vs original model 96.56%; best among unlearning methods
Common-robustness ASR (Table 7): SafeRedir I2P 0.70% (best), MMA 1.73% (2nd to AdvUnlearn 1.03%)
Adversarial ASR under UnlearnDiffAtk (Table 8): SafeRedir NSFW 9.38% (AdvUnlearn best 4.69%), Van Gogh 50.16% (best), Church 3.12% (best); highest avg attack time e.g. 340.87s on NSFW
Adaptive detector-aware attack (Fig. 12): NSFW ASR stays 1.56%-7.81% across delta sweep, worst case at delta=0.8
Detector-modality ablation (Table 3): Text&Lat&t overall/any-step = 99.73/99.98% on IGMU and 74.72/99.60% on MMA, vs text-only 89.17/51.68% overall
Redirection-config ablation (Table 4): naive pairwise emb1 FSR 14.27% / LPIPS 0.51; latent-aware emb2 alone FSR 60.40% / LPIPS 0.26; emb2+alpha=2.0 FSR 96.80% / LPIPS 0.31
Transferability (Table 9): SD v1.4-trained module applied zero-shot to 6 variants keeps FSR >94%, often >99% on NSFW
Composability (Fig. 13 / Sec. 5.3.2): stacking SafeRedir raises weak baselines' FSR by 6.83-50.54% and cuts NSFW ASR by avg 44.47% (up to 82.81% on ConceptPrune)
Efficiency (Sec. 5.2.5): 50MB module, <1.5% inference-latency increase for unsafe prompts
